title: phleum pratense subsp. alpinum
intro: Phleum pratense subsp. alpinum, also known as alpine timothy grass, is a subspecies of the perennial grass Phleum pratense, native to the mountainous regions of Europe, Asia, and North Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of alpine timothy grass include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for conditions such as bronchitis and allergies. Alpine timothy grass can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or used topically to promote health and well-being.
